title: plantago sempervirens var. asperrima
intro: Plantago sempervirens var. asperrima, also known as the rough-leaved plantain or prickly plantain, is a perennial plant in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) characterized by its broad, flat leaves and small, greenish-yellow flowers. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of compounds such as aucubin and plantamajoside. Consuming Plantago sempervirens var. asperrima has been associated with various health benefits, including the relief of coughs, colds, and respiratory issues, as well as the promotion of wound healing and the reduction of swelling. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
